days.title: Intro
days.description: Mastering the loop between human intent and AI execution.
days.mindset: You are the Architect. The LLM is your Project Manager. Project IDX is your Lead Developer. You don't need to speak the machine's language fluently; you just need to guide the conversation.

exercises.name: 1. The Kickstart: AI Blueprinting
exercises.note: Describe your vision in plain English to generate a No-Code Implementation Plan.

exercises.name: 2. The Implementation: Anti-Gravity (IDX)
exercises.note: Feed your plan into Project IDX to generate the initial codebase and see a tangible preview instantly.

exercises.name: 3. The Review: Strategic Feedback
exercises.note: Identify changes and describe adjustments back to an LLM to generate a Focused Update Plan.

exercises.name: 4. The Iteration: Refine and Repeat
exercises.note: Move back and forth between the plan and implementation until the project perfectly matches your vision.

days.title: Snippets
days.description: Copy and paste these templates to accelerate your Vibe Coding workflow.

exercises.name: Request an Implementation Plan
exercises.content:
Create an implementation plan for this update, called '##_IMPLEMENTATION_.md'
- Add this file to the implementation_plans folder, create one if needed

Don't change any code yet, only create that file, also include a checklist so we can track our progress as we go, on this or other chats with AI dev agents.
exercises.note: Append this to the end of any new request. Replace ## with the next number in the sequence.

exercises.name: Update an Implementation Plan
exercises.content:
Update the implementation plan for this update, called '##_IMPLEMENTATION_.md'

Don't change any code yet, only create that file, also include a checklist so we can track our progress as we go, on this or other chats with AI dev agents.
exercises.note: Append this when revisiting an existing plan. Replace ## with the plan number to update.

exercises.name: Execute the Plan
exercises.content: Great. Let's implement that, update the checklist as you go. @
exercises.note: Replace @ with the plan file reference. Execute

days.title: Clone
days.description: Follow these steps to clone any website using AI with pixel-perfect accuracy.
notes: Bolt.new usage is limited; you can create approximately 4 clones before hitting limits.
Use Bolt strictly to build the initial production version containing ONLY the navigation and hero sections.
Transition to AntiGravity + Claude to iteratively generate all remaining sections.

exercises.name: Capture the Vision
exercises.note: On the target webpage, use a full-page screenshot tool to save the entire layout as a PNG file. Capture everything — header to footer.

exercises.name: Optimize Assets
exercises.note: Upload your PNG to Squoosh and convert it to WebP. This compresses the image without sacrificing quality, keeping it within AI token limits.

exercises.name: The Bolt Clone Prompt
exercises.note: Open Bolt.new, upload your WebP image, and paste this expert prompt. Bolt will build the UI iteratively, starting with the nav and hero.
exercises.content:
You are an expert UI/UX Frontend Developer tasked with creating a pixel-perfect, front-end-only React/Tailwind UI clone. You will work iteratively, building one section at a time to ensure maximum code quality and avoid token limits.

## Technology Stack Requirements:
- React (Vite) with TypeScript
- Tailwind CSS for all styling
- Lucide React for icons only

## Critical Implementation Guidelines:

### 1. Visual Accuracy Requirements:
- Analyze the provided screenshot(s) with extreme attention to detail
- Replicate exact layout, structural hierarchy, typography, color palette, and spacing
- Match padding, margins, font sizes, and visual proportions precisely
- Preserve the exact visual hierarchy and component relationships

### 2. Technical Constraints:
- Front-end only: No backend, databases, or authentication logic
- Mock data: Create realistic, contextually appropriate mock data arrays (avoid generic Lorem Ipsum)
- Responsive design: Implement flawless mobile, tablet, and desktop breakpoints using Tailwind's responsive prefixes
- Interactive elements: Add hover states, focus rings, and smooth transitions for all interactive components
- Assets: Use lucide-react icons and high-quality Unsplash URLs for images (format: https://images.unsplash.com/photo-...)

### 3. Code Quality Standards:
- Write production-ready, modular TypeScript components
- Use proper TypeScript interfaces and types
- Implement reusable components where applicable
- Follow React best practices and hooks patterns
- Ensure accessibility with proper ARIA labels and semantic HTML

## Phase 1 Execution Instructions:
Step 1: Analyze the visual design and establish foundational app structure
Step 2: Configure any custom Tailwind settings (colors, fonts, spacing) based on the screenshot
Step 3: Build ONLY the Navigation Bar and Hero Section with complete functionality
Step 4: Stop and request direction for the next section

## Critical Constraints:
- Build ONLY the header/navigation and hero section in this phase
- Do NOT create placeholder comments for future sections
- Do NOT attempt to build the entire page
- Provide complete, functional code for the specified sections only
- After completion, ask: "Which section should we build next?"

URL: []

exercises.name: Export to GitHub
exercises.note: Once satisfied with the Bolt output, connect your project to GitHub. This preserves your work, enables version control, and unlocks deployment options.

exercises.name: Refine on AntiGravity
exercises.note: Pull the repository into AntiGravity for surgical adjustments. Use implementation plans to guide the AI through precise, targeted edits until the clone is perfect.
